front: Sculpture: series of hinged aluminum sculptures meant to be manipulated by viewers; title means 'critters'
Work: cutting a Mobius strip lengthwise repeatedly until it's too thin to cut
Work: two participants bind their hands together with a Mobius strip; part of the Nostalgia of the Body series
back: Nostalgia of the Body series (Lygia Clark)

front: Work: participants slowly remove yarn from their mouths
Artist: wrote 'Nostalgia of the Body,' defining Neo-Concretism as treating art as an 'almost-body'
Artist: co-founded Neo-Concrete movement in Brazil with Helio Oiticica and Lygia Pape; inspired by 1951 Max Bill retrospective
Artist: painting Unity No. 1 depicts a square with a white border projecting from the wall
Artist: shares first name 'Lygia' with Lygia Pape (creator of Divisor); works shown at MASP in São Paulo
